feedback: Recharge plan validity is too short for the price. Bad deal overall.
Standard. No complaints but no praise either.
Customer support resolved my query in under five minutes.

Internet speed meets expectations for the plan I have chosen. It is neither impressively fast nor frustratingly slow. Data balance consumption seems proportional to my actual usage which is reassuring. Overall a middling experience that does what it needs to do.
omg cant blv how bad the value for money has become. same plan that was 299 last year is now 349 with fewer ott apps and less data. they think we wont notice but we do. terrible strategy

The recharge plans need a revamp. The current structure is outdated and does not reflect how people actually consume content and data today. Most users want high data limits with good OTT access rather than unlimited calls which are barely used anymore.
Called about a billing error and the support agent corrected it immediately.
cant blv how bad the call quality is here.

The recharge plan structure is well thought out with clear pricing tiers. Each level up adds meaningful benefits like better OTT access, more data, and longer validity. The mobile app comparison tool makes choosing easy and billing always reflects exactly what was advertised.
Called support about my reacharg not getting activated. agent was helpful and fixed it in 2 mins. good experience this time unlike previous calls where they kept me waiting
custmer care was rude and unhelpful. very disappointing experience.

Horrible network coverage on highways between the two cities. My call dropped 5 times during a 2 hour drive yesterday. This is unacceptable for people who commute daily. I need a reliable connection for work calls and this network simply cannot deliver that basic requirement on major roads.
omg worst data balance tracking. shows wrong numbers always.

I appreciate the flexible recharge plan options available now. Whether you need a weekly top-up or an annual commitment plan, there is something at every price point. The billing is always accurate and the app makes it easy to compare plans before recharging.

Positive experience with the callback feature. No waiting on hold anymore. I registered for a callback and received a call within 15 minutes from a well-trained agent who resolved my query about value for money plans efficiently. This is much better than the old system.

How nice of them to provide 5G coverage in exactly two spots in the entire city. I just need to stand on the corner of MG Road and 5th Cross to experience the future. Very convenient for all of us who live there permanently on that specific corner.
reacharg plan options are confusing. too many similar choices.
Decent internet speed on most days. Acceptable for my work.

The combination of fast 5G internet speed, comprehensive OTT bundle, and competitive pricing on the 799 plan makes this carrier my top choice. I stream in 4K daily without buffering and the data allocation is generous enough that I never worry about running out before the validity period ends.
Network coverage in the suburbs is acceptable. Standard availability.
Value for money is acceptable on this plan. Standard.

The customer support experience on the priority line has been consistently excellent throughout my two years as a premium subscriber. Agents are knowledgeable, wait times are minimal, and issues get resolved on the first call. This alone justifies the higher pricing of the premium plan for me.

Value for money is neither good nor bad on this carrier currently. They sit right in the middle of the market in terms of what you pay versus what you receive. Billing accuracy is good which I appreciate. The recharge plans could offer slightly more data to stand out.

The network coverage is wide but the call quality doesn't match. I get full signal bars but voice calls still have a slight delay which is annoying during professional conversations. The 5G data speeds are good though so I use VoIP instead.
Data balance tracking in the app is functional. The mobile app itself is well-designed. Data validity info is displayed clearly and recharge options are standard.

I raised a complaint about network coverage issues three weeks ago and customer support has not provided any update since. Every time I follow up they say it is being worked on but nothing changes. The lack of accountability is infuriating for someone paying a premium.

I find it charming that the mobile app has a speed test feature built in that consistently shows 150 Mbps while actual browsing feels like dial-up internet. The cognitive dissonance is impressive. At least the recharge flow works without crashing most of the time.

Roaming worked perfectly in the Middle East. Call quality was clear and data speeds were sufficient for WhatsApp video calls. The per-day pricing was transparent and there were no hidden charges on my bill when I returned. A worry-free international experience.

The complete package of fast 5G internet speed, reliable network coverage, clear call quality, and fair pricing makes this carrier my top recommendation. Add the comprehensive OTT bundle and generous data validity and you have genuine value for money that I have not found elsewhere.
Data validity expired while I still had 5GB remaining. Wasted money.

The 5G experience at the airport and surrounding business district is genuinely world-class. Speed tests consistently show over 900 Mbps and the low latency makes real-time collaboration tools work flawlessly. Downloaded a complete three-hour movie in under a minute while waiting for my flight. Technology working as intended.

The coverage along my daily 40-kilometer commute route through multiple zones is consistent and reliable. I pass through urban, suburban, and semi-rural areas with functional connectivity maintained throughout the entire drive. Call quality is adequate for hands-free conversations and speeds are reasonable for background streaming during the commute.

SIM activation was delayed because of a system error on their end. The app showed my documents were verified but the backend did not process it for two extra days. Only resolved after I contacted support directly. The app status was misleading the whole time.

Really good value for money compared to other carriers in the market. I have tried three different providers in the last two years and this one offers the most balanced combination of speed, coverage, and price. The recharge plans are competitively priced.

The IVR system is terrible and customer support is impossible to reach directly. You have to navigate through 7 menu options before you can speak to a human. By the time someone picks up you have already wasted 15 minutes. The actual agents are fine but the system makes it painful.
reacharg plans have become too expensive for middle class users. 249 for basic plan is not affordable when u compare with other options available in market. billing also confusing sometimes
Recharge plan with OTT is fine. Average offering for the price.
plz hire more custmer care agents. every time i call its 20-30 min wait. the agents themselves are fine once u get through but getting through is the prblm. very irritating for urgent issues
SIM activated same day. Quick and efficient process overall.
Nothing special. It is a phone network.

The new 299 recharge plan gives you 2GB daily data with 28 days validity and unlimited calls. For the price this is genuinely the best deal available right now. I compared across all major carriers and no one matches this combination of data and call benefits.
Recharge plan with standard data allocation. Pricing is market average.
Support team is adequate. They handle complaints at normal pace.
Overcharged on my last bill. Waiting for resolution from support.
5G speeds are there but nothing mind-blowing. Acceptable I guess.
cant blv they charge extra for caller tune when it used to be free. billing has hidden charges everywhere now. v bad practice

Terrible call quality in basement areas and underground parking. I expected better from a premium carrier that charges top dollar for their plans. Every time I go below ground level the calls become completely unusable with heavy static and frequent disconnections. This is a basic infrastructure issue they need to address.

I appreciate that the recharge plans clearly state which OTT services are included before purchase. The app shows a comparison view that makes it easy to decide. Value for money improves significantly on the quarterly plans compared to monthly ones. Smart to commit longer.

The roaming service was a complete disaster during my recent trip to Southeast Asia. Despite activating the international pack three days before departure the service never connected to partner networks abroad. Customer support suggested manually selecting networks which did not work either. Ended up buying a local SIM.
reacharg plans need more data at current price points offered.
Mobile app size is too large. Takes up too much phone storage.

The billing practices on this carrier are standard and transparent. Charges are listed clearly, amounts match what I expect based on my plan, and payment processing works normally. Average billing experience consistent with industry norms without notable positives or negatives.
omg the customer support wait time is insane. 45 minutes just to speak to someone about a simple reacharg issue. by the time they answered i forgot what i was calling about lol
Fair pricing for the 5G plans. Early adopter friendly rates.
Roaming experience in Dubai was average. Internet worked decently enough.
Smooth 5G experience on my new phone. Very fast downloads.
Their 5G coverage is basically non-existent outside metro cities. Do not waste your money on premium plans if you live in smaller towns.
Recharge plan variety is adequate. Covers basic to premium needs.

omg the sim activation process is ridiculous. submitted documents 5 days ago and still pending. called custmer care twice and they just say wait. meanwhile i have no working phone number. worst onboarding ever

The pricing on the new unlimited plans is actually competitive now. They seem to have listened to customer feedback and brought rates in line with market expectations. I compared the top three carriers and this one edges ahead slightly on overall value for money.

I had to get a replacement SIM after my phone was stolen and the activation process was surprisingly quick. The store verified my identity, issued the new SIM, and I was back online within an hour. Data balance and plan were all intact on the new SIM.

The billing system on this carrier has been consistent throughout my three years of service. Every charge is itemized, there are no unexpected fees, and the auto-debit works every cycle. Standard process that functions as expected without any notable issues or surprises.

The roaming experience was terrible. No connectivity in Japan despite activating the pack. Call quality was non-existent and customer support took three days to even acknowledge the issue. Ended up buying a local SIM which defeats the purpose of having an international plan.
Terrible internet speed since morning. Cannot even send emails.
Network coverage okay. Internet and call quality also average.

The self-service options on the app have reduced my need to contact customer support entirely. I can check my data balance, change my recharge plan, and view billing history all without waiting on hold. When I did need human help, the chat was responsive.
omg the custmer care is so incompetent. asked them a simple question about data balance and got transferred 3 times. each person gave different answer. nobody knows anything there
OTT bundle activation through the app works. Standard process.
prblm with roaming charges on my bill. activated pack through app but got charged separately for data. custmer care says its a system error but wont refund. very frustrting experience

The escalation process in customer support works well. When the first level agent could not solve my persistent call quality issue she immediately connected me with a technical specialist who ran diagnostics remotely and identified a tower configuration problem in my area.
plz explain why my data balance finished in 1 day. i only used whatsapp and checked email. this is clearly a system error on ur end. want immediate resolution

The value for money proposition completely falls apart on the basic plans below 299. No OTT access, minimal data, short validity. It feels like they deliberately cripple the lower tiers to push people toward expensive plans. Budget users deserve decent value too.

Excellent customer support experience today. The agent was empathetic and resolved my concern about unexpected billing charges within a single interaction. She explained each charge clearly and reversed the ones that were incorrectly applied. This is the standard all telecom support should aim for.

The internet speed and data validity on my current plan are both perfectly adequate for my usage level. I browse social media watch some videos and use maps for navigation daily. Never ran into balance issues and the 28-day validity works with my monthly budget cycle.

I have been facing persistent network coverage issues in my residential area for over two months now. Despite raising three separate complaint tickets and speaking with customer support each time, there has been zero improvement. Calls drop constantly and the internet speed is unusable during evening hours when everyone in the colony is online.
Pricing could be better but it is what it is.
Recharge plans are okay for basic usage. Fair enough pricing.

Called customer support to understand the pricing difference between two recharge plans. The agent explained everything clearly and even recommended a plan that better suited my usage pattern which saved me money. Rare to find support staff who actually care about the customer.
Upgraded to 5G and speed improvement is noticeable. Data validity of 84 days is standard. Value for money and pricing are average despite small price increase.
intrenet speed is slow but the recharge plan pricing is fair for what you get. billing is also correct every time so no complaints on the money side of things
5G experience is okay in my area. Sometimes fast sometimes regular.
App works fine for recharges. No complaints on that.
Customer support is hard to reach. Long wait times always.
omg the billing is so messed up. random charges appearing every month that nobody can explain. called custmer care 4 times still no proper explanation. switching carriers next month for sure

I contacted customer support about my internet speed dropping to unusable levels every evening. The agent not only logged the complaint but also escalated it to the technical team who called me back within two hours with a fix. The speed is back to normal now. Excellent service.

Have been using the 5G service for two months now. Network coverage inside buildings could be better but outdoor reception is strong. The app makes it easy to track my data consumption and the recharge process is seamless.
Recharge options are okay. Satisfactory for average users.

plz give us the option to choose which ott apps we want in our bundle. i dont use prime but would love youtube premium instead. customizable bundles would be much better value for different types of users

I want to highlight how the carrier's investment in infrastructure has transformed my daily connectivity experience over the past year. The 5G tower installed near my home delivers consistent speeds above 500 Mbps, call quality is impeccable, and the OTT streaming experience is buffer-free even at peak hours. Genuine improvement.

I purchased the international roaming pack for my business trip to Europe but it simply did not activate upon landing despite multiple attempts through the app. Spent two days abroad without any connectivity until I found WiFi to contact customer support who said it was a provisioning error. Completely unacceptable.
Billing cycle runs on auto-debit. Works as expected.

Called customer support about my 5G not working after switching plans. The agent immediately identified the configuration issue and pushed new settings to my phone over the air. Within five minutes my 5G was working perfectly. Best support call I have ever made.

I want to appreciate the significant network improvements in my locality over the past six months. Earlier the coverage was extremely spotty and calls would frequently drop mid-conversation which was very embarrassing during work calls. After they installed a new tower last quarter everything has improved dramatically. Calls are crystal clear now and I rarely see signal drops even indoors.

The network coverage inside my office building has improved significantly after they installed a small cell in our complex last month. I now get strong signal on every floor including the basement conference room. Call quality is consistently excellent which was previously my biggest frustration.
Good plan with OTT included. Worth the monthly investment.
Pricing on the student plan is average. Similar to other carriers.

omg cant blv the sim activation is still pending after 6 days. customer support gives different answer every time. one says 24 hrs another says 72 hrs. meanwhile no phone service at all. completely unacceptable in 2024
Recharge plans are overpriced. Competitors are much cheaper.
prblm with ott bundle is that it only works on one device. cant share the netflix login with family even though its supposed to be a family plan. misleading marketing and poor value for money

I am incredibly frustrated that the 5G service I am paying premium prices for barely works in my area despite their coverage map showing full availability. On an average day I get 5G signal for maybe two hours and the rest of the time it falls back to 4G. The coverage map is clearly misleading customers into expensive plans.
Customer support was okay. Standard interaction nothing special there.
OTT content quality in the bundle is basic tier. Not premium access.
Data balance seems fine. Matches my usage pattern.
Value for money has dropped since last year. Plans give less now.

The internet speed degradation in my locality over the past quarter has been dramatic and unaddressed. What used to be reliable 80 Mbps has dwindled to 10-15 Mbps during most of the day. I suspect tower overloading as many new apartments have come up nearby but the carrier has not scaled infrastructure to match demand.
plz fix ur network coverage in downtown. cant even make a call without it dropping. v bad experience tbh
plz improve coverage in my area. been 6 months no improvement. calls drop daily
Not bad not good. Just there.
5G availability in my area is inconsistent. Works sometimes.
Roaming. It happened. I survived.
Recharge plan benefits are satisfactory for the cost involved.
Average 5G. Average coverage. Nothing exceptional.
Mobile app is functional. Does what it needs to do.
The 5G in this city is amazing. Never going back to my old provider. Speeds are unreal.
Data validity is decent. 56 days works for me.
Data balance and validity both working as expected. Straightforward.

The mobile app experience is functional since the last update. Previously it would crash during recharges but that is fixed now. The data balance display works and the plan comparison feature exists. It does what it needs to do without being particularly impressive or problematic.
OTT bundles are useless. Only gives basic plans with limited content.
Pricing is reasonable for the service provided. Fair enough.

Very happy with the internet speed and data balance transparency on this network. I switched two months ago and the improvement over my previous carrier is night and day. Data validity of 84 days on my plan means I only recharge four times a year which is very convenient.
reacharg plans r too costly for the network we get. custmer care doesnt help either

The internet speed drops significantly after I cross 1GB daily limit. The throttled speed is practically unusable for anything beyond basic messaging. I understand fair usage policies but throttling to 64kbps is too aggressive. At least give us 512kbps post limit.

The network coverage along the Chennai-Bangalore highway is adequate for my weekly drive. I maintain connectivity for calls and basic data usage throughout most of the route. Standard coverage with occasional brief gaps in some stretches but nothing that significantly impacts my commute.
Data validity of 28 days is standard. Normal market offering.
the reacharg plan i took has poor data validity and the network coverage in my area is below average. not getting value for the money spent at all

The internet speed throttling after daily limit is so aggressive that the connection becomes completely unusable. At 64 kbps even loading a basic text email takes forever. This punitive approach to post-limit speed is outdated when competitors offer at least 1 Mbps after limit which is enough for messaging and light browsing.
Internet speed is pathetically slow. Cannot even load basic webpages.
OTT bundle included in my plan is standard. Covers basic streaming.
Mobile app is decent. Does the basic job without issues.
v bad value for money on this network. paying 399 per month and getting pathetic speeds. the reacharg plans look good on paper but actual experience is very different

Excellent recharge plan options for every budget. Very satisfied with what I get for the price. The annual plan in particular offers outstanding value and the billing is always straightforward without any hidden surprises at the end of the cycle.
Pricing keeps increasing without any service improvement visible.
ngl the customer support has improved a lot recently. got my issue resolved in under 5 minutes which never used to happen before. maybe they hired better people
Billing error again this month. Extra 99 charged randomly.
SIM activation happened on time. Network started working as expected.
tbh ok speed for the price. data validity could be longer though. 28 days feels short for the amount they charge
omg the app update broke everything. cant login cant recharge cant even see my plan details. been like this for 2 days now. plz fix immediately this is basic functionality
cant blv how expensive the plans have become. used to be the affordable option but now pricing is same as premium carriers without the premium service quality

After switching to this carrier last month the overall experience has been average. The internet speed is adequate for most tasks, the network coverage is present in areas where my previous provider struggled, and the recharge plan pricing is in line with what competitors charge in the market.

Excellent 5G speeds in the tech park area. Internet is blazing fast for all my work needs including large file transfers and HD video conferences. The recharge plan at 599 gives me enough data daily and the OTT bundle is a welcome bonus.

Roaming in the US was excellent on this carrier. I had consistent 4G speeds across multiple states and the daily pack at 499 rupees gave me enough data for maps navigation and video calls. The app tracked my usage in real-time which helped me avoid overcharges.

The value proposition has clearly eroded over time. When I first joined three years ago this was the most affordable carrier with the best plans. Now the pricing is at par with premium carriers but the service quality remains budget level. Very disappointed with the direction.
Billing needs improvement. Too many unexplained small charges.

The mobile app UX for activating roaming packs is needlessly complicated requiring seven taps and two confirmation screens to complete what should be a one-step process. I almost missed activating before my flight because the final confirmation page kept timing out. For a travel-essential feature this friction is unacceptable design.
Call quality is neither good nor bad. Average experience overall.
Service. That is all.

The roaming service worked perfectly across my entire two-week business trip covering four Asian countries. Network switching between partner carriers was automatic and the speed remained acceptable for email and video calls throughout. Very impressed with the seamless connectivity.

The pricing revision they did last quarter was actually customer-friendly for once. They reduced the cost of the popular 2GB plan by 20 rupees while maintaining all benefits. The new recharge plan at 179 is genuinely good value and the billing reflects the correct amount without issues.
Dropped calls twice today. Network needs serious improvement here.

Network is average and gets the job done for basic everyday calls and light browsing. I have not experienced complete outages but the speeds are never fast enough for smooth video calls or heavy streaming. For the price I pay it feels like just barely acceptable service. Nothing that makes me want to stay or switch.
